LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT PLANT INSULATION COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. RULING: NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED AS TO THE CLAIMS FOR FALSE REPRESENTATION, OWNER/CONTRACTOR LIABILITY, AND PUNITIVE DAMAGES. DEFENDANT SHIFTED THE BURDEN OF PRODUCING EVIDENCE WHEN PLAINTIFF'S DEPOSITION TESTIMONY AND RESPONSES TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES ARE CONSIDERED. PLAINTIFF HAS PROVIDED NO BASIS FOR THE COURT TO CONCLUDE THAT PLAINTIFF'S RECOLLECTION WAS REFRESHED AFTER HIS DEPOSITION. THE MOTION IS CONTINUED TO JANUARY 22, 2009 FOR DEFENDANT TO BE GIVEN THE OPPORTUNITY TO DEPOSE MR. MUHILLY, GIVEN THAT PLAINTIFF PREVIOUSLY DECLINED TO PRODUCE HIM FOR A DEPOSITION. CALVILLO'S TESTIMONY IN A PRIOR CASE IS NOT ADMISSIBLE IN CONNECTION WITH THIS MOTION. (302/PJM) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

ASBESTOS MOTION CALENDAR ON MAY-11-2006 IN 206, PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL WAS GRANTED PURSUANT TO CCP 36(A). THE COURT SEVERED CASE FROM BRAYTON GROUP 381 AND VACATED THE PREVIOUS TRIAL DATE OF 1/8/07 AND MSC OF 12/29/06. THE TRIAL DATE IS SET FOR 8/21/06 @ 11:15AM IN DEPT. 206; SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE SET FOR 8/11/06 @ 9:30AM IN DEPT. 304. DISCOVERY IS OPEN UNTIL THE CASE IS ASSIGNED TO A TRIAL COURT. G.O. 140 RE: MOTIONS FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT STIPULATED TO. PLAINTIFF SHALL DISCLOSE ALL PRODUCT IDENTIFICATION AND CAUSATION WITNESSES AS TO EACH DEFENDANT BY 6/23/06, AND SHALL ALSO PRODUCE DOCUMENTS THEY INTEND TO USE AT TRIAL AS TO PRODUCT IDENTIFICATION AS TO EACH DEFENDANT BY THAT DATE. IF PLAINTIFF LEARNS OF NEW WITNESSES, IN GOOD FAITH, THEY SHALL IMMEDIATELY BE DISCLOSED ALONG WITH DECLARATION AS TO WHY THIS DISCLOSURE WAS NOT PROVIDED ON THE DUE DATE. AN ECONOMIC REPORT AND/OR OFFER OF THEIR EXPERT ECONOMIST FOR DEPOSITION WILL BE SERVED BY FAX NO LATER THAN 14 DAYS BEFORE THE TRIAL DATE. THE PARED-DOWN LIST OF EXPERTS WILL BE FAXED NO LATER THAN 14 DAYS BEFORE TRIAL DATE. NO LATER THAN 12 DAYS BEFORE THE TRIAL DATE, EACH SIDE MAY IDENTIFY UP TO THREE EXPERT WITNESSES PERCEIVED TO BE NECESSARY TO BE DEPOSED FOR MEANINGFUL SETTLEMENT DISCUSSIONS TO OCCUR. THESE EXPERTS WILL BE DEPOSED PRIOR TO THE TRIAL DATE. TRIAL WILL NOT BE DELAYED FOR THE PURPOSE OF DEPOSING EXPERTS. ORDER SIGNED.
